title: passerina annua
intro: Passerina annua, also known as the annual blue pasque flower, is a flowering plant native to the prairies of the Great Plains in North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which are attributed to its alkaloids and saponins. The health benefits of Passerina annua are said to include the ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The plant is typically harvested for its roots and dried for use in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures.
